value: Strategies must be agent-actionable: judgment-heavy steps need explicit escalation paths. The pattern is (a) concrete question to ask, (b) what to do when clear, (c) what analysis to do when unclear, (d) present framed options to human. Agents should never ask open questions like 'where should this go?' — instead do the analysis, group collaborating elements, name the concept, and present: 'these three types cluster around pricing logic, I'd call this a pricing boundary.' This applies to all layer artifacts, not just strategies.

Strategy-editor needs three updates from sessions: (1) Agent-actionability quality check — can an agent follow steps as a protocol from step 1 without assumed context? Caught planning gap in Tidy or Change. (2) Layer-boundary heuristic — if a clarification applies across all languages, it belongs in strategy rationale, not per-language divergence docs. (3) Coherence check gap — outcomes must trace to the PRIMARY principle. If an outcome primarily serves a different principle, reframe it to show how it serves the primary, or move it to rationale as a noted benefit. Caught when an outcome served GDB instead of FEAL.

Strategy-editor update: Add outcome↔principle alignment sub-check under coherence. Each outcome must trace to the PRIMARY principle. If an outcome primarily serves a different principle, reframe it to show how it serves the primary, or move it to rationale as a noted benefit. Catches silent failure mode where outcomes pass all existing checks but serve wrong principle. Decided against agent-actionability check (#1, just good reviewing not a formalizable rule) and layer-boundary heuristic (#3, tautological). Only #2 is worth adding.
